subject: Physics
chapter: Exploring the Investigative World of Science

key_points: Science is based on careful observation, questioning, and investigation
Scientific investigations begin with curiosity and questions about natural phenomena
Scientists form hypotheses which are testable explanations
Experiments are conducted to test hypotheses under controlled conditions
Recording and analyzing data helps scientists draw conclusions
Scientific results should be repeatable and verifiable by others
examples: Investigating whether plants grow faster in sunlight or shade
Observing how sugar dissolves faster in warm water than cold water
Studying how a puri puffs when heated
Testing which material conducts heat faster
formulas: Scientific investigation cycle = Observation + Experiment + Analysis
Reliable result = Repeatable experiment + Accurate observations
exam_tips: Understand the difference between observation and inference
Remember the steps of the scientific method
Use clear examples when explaining scientific investigation
Always connect experiments with the hypothesis being tested
